purpose: Reference data for re-running athlete-name normalization after scraper fixes.

primary_artifacts.description: Snapshot of all unique athlete names currently present in 2-normalize-athletes, intended as the main canonical-name lookup source.
Metadata for the canonical athlete name snapshot.

format_rules.suffix_rule: Last, First Jr|Sr|II|III|IV with no periods
format_rules.live_scan_rule: If a conflict appears in a fresh scan, treat it as unresolved regardless of prior memory.

scraper_assumptions_for_next_pass.implication: Variant-to-canonical mappings from the old pipeline may be less reusable than the canonical-name snapshot itself.

workflow_rules.when_placing_is_zero: A 0-placing overall winner should match an existing 1-placing name in the same file whenever possible.
workflow_rules.overall_winner_propagation_rule: When a 0-placing athlete matches a 1-placing athlete in the same file, rewrite the 0 line to exactly mirror the normalized 1 name.
workflow_rules.unmatched_zero_rule: If a 0-placing athlete cannot be matched to a 1-placing athlete in the same file after smart normalization-aware comparison, flag it for manual review.

explicit_name_decisions.notes: East Asian name plus appended western given name uses comma before western name and no @ prefix.
Confirmed by social media; do not merge with Mchan, Carlos.
Do not force into Mc... capitalization rules.
User confirmed this spelling; an earlier 'McKenzi' mention was a typo.
Preferred East Asian format; earlier Hosoo/Joo reversal normalized here.
Confirmed by social media as the correct order.
User indicated this form is correct as-is.
Keep in western format, not @ east Asian format.
Keep in western format, not @ east Asian format.
Keep in western format, not @ east Asian format.
Keep in western format, not @ east Asian format.
Keep in western format, not @ east Asian format.

notes: The canonical snapshot is more important than the old variant list because the scraper behavior changed.
A future reprocessing pass should use canonical-athlete-names-2026-04-02.jsonl as a candidate lookup table, not as automatic ground truth.
The files in 2-normalize-athletes contain a mixture of vetted and unvetted names.
Only names that were part of reviewed conflict clusters were fully vetted during the prior cleanup process.
Names that never appeared in a cluster were not necessarily reviewed and should not be assumed canonical just because they appear in the snapshot.
Because the scraper has changed and now includes previously missed results while excluding some prior bad results, cluster membership may differ in the new dataset.
Some earlier normalized files may still contain stale or incorrect records from scraper bugs; the snapshot is a strong starting point, not infallible ground truth.
